title: Germany: Ukrainian national charged over Nord Stream blasts
summary: A Ukrainian national has been charged in relation to a series of explosions which severely damaged the Nord Stream gas pipeline in the Baltic Sea in 2022.

title: Hospital fire in northern Germany kills two
summary: A fire at a hospital in the northern German town of Ludwigslust has killed two people. But preliminary reports of dozens of injured appear to have been false.

title: German prosecutors arrest Rwanda genocide suspect
summary: A German-Rwandan national has been arrested on suspicion of complicity in murders during Rwanda's 1994 genocide. He is thought to have been personally involved in the killing of one victim.

title: Germany news: Raids on DFB, host cities over Euro 2024 perks
summary: Police have raided DFB offices and city halls in a probe into the alleged misappropriation of Euro 2024 tickets. Meanwhile, Germany’s coalition leaders are meeting to hammer out a reform package. Follow DW for more.

title: Germany news: Stade attack victims yet to be identified
summary: Police have yet to identify the six victims of a deadly shooting in the northern German city. Meanwhile, Germany's national men's football coach pledged to stay on the job after an early World Cup exit. DW has more.

title: Germany's Merz and soccer: A PR move that backfired
summary: "You've inspired us," read a post by Chancellor Merz on X right after Germany's lackluster performance and early elimination from the FIFA World Cup. It is seen as a communication blunder that has repercussions.

title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?
summary: A quarter of German youth spend too much time on social media, with 350,000 considered to have an addiction problem. Education Minister Karin Prien is advocating for a legal minimum age of 13.

title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.
